models_tested.notes: 28 evals × 2 configurations = 56 runs, all run_number == 1. Eval 26 (unsupported-model-error) result fields nulled on both with_skill and without_skill sides due to without_skill contamination (executor read SKILL.md from filesystem); excluded from run_summary aggregates. Sonnet pass-rate delta computed over 27 paired evals (eval 26 excluded). Time/token coverage is sparse: 7 of 27 paired primary runs measured (evals 1, 3, 4, 11, 12, 13, 14); the remaining evals use simulated transcripts or have measurements excluded as stale pre-v1.3 data.

notes: Assertion 'spawns subagent' fails in both configurations for evals 1 and 4 because the Agent tool is not available inside eval executor subagents. This is an eval harness constraint, not a skill defect.

Eval 2 (consistency-mode-plan-tasks-mismatch) re-run in v1.4 with updated assertions (+0.40 delta): enters-consistency-mode, finds-missing-verbose-task, and apply-prompt-shown differentiate with_skill from without_skill. (Earlier version of this note referenced spec mode detection, which was removed in v1.3.)
Eval 4 (diff mode) is the secondary discriminating eval (+0.20 delta): explicit diff mode declaration differentiates, subagent assertion fails in both due to harness constraint.
Eval 3 (argument-conflict-error) is non-discriminating: both configurations correctly detect the mutually exclusive target conflict and output the error without running a review.
Eval 1 is zero-delta (0.80/0.80) due to harness constraint masking the subagent-spawning differentiator.

Evals 5-10 test Phase II multi-LLM routing. Eval 6 (copilot-empty-findings) is non-discriminating: empty findings → 'No issues found.' is natural default behavior. Evals 5, 7, 8, 9, 10 all discriminate.
Evals 5-10 use simulated transcripts (fixture responses embedded in prompts); time_seconds and tokens are null for these runs.

Evals 11-14 added in v1.1 review pass. Eval 13 (focus-option) discriminates (+0.67 delta): the skill-defined focus line format and apply prompt both fail without_skill. Evals 11, 12, 14 are non-discriminating: empty-staged warning, PR metadata inclusion (when fixture data is explicit in the prompt), and skip handling are natural baseline behaviors.
Evals 11-14 have real time and token measurements (subagent executor ran the full workflow, not pure transcript simulation).
Evals 15-20 added in v1.2 for triage layer and post-apply re-scan. All use simulated fixtures; time_seconds and tokens are null.

Evals 15 (triage-skips-false-positive) and 16 (triage-all-skipped) and 19 (rescan-offered-after-apply) are discriminating. Evals 17, 18, 20 are non-discriminating: triage-not-on-self-path and rescan-not-offered-after-skip are natural baseline behaviors; triage-user-includes-skipped passes because 'S1' literal selection is intuitive.

Evals 2, 21, 22, 23 added/re-run in v1.4. Eval 2 (consistency-mode-plan-tasks-mismatch) re-run with v1.3-compatible assertions — discriminating (+0.40 delta). Eval 21 (both-staged-and-unstaged-prompt) discriminates (+0.67 delta): disambiguation prompt is entirely skill-defined. Evals 22 (unstaged-only-auto-review) and 23 (staged-explicit-bypasses-detection) are non-discriminating: these behaviors are intuitive enough that the baseline handles them correctly. Adding evals 22 and 23 diluted the headline delta from +0.30 to +0.29.

Summary-table delta values are computed from unrounded means over 27 paired evals (eval 26 excluded from both sides due to contamination; with_skill=0.97778, without_skill=0.71481), so they may differ slightly from subtracting the displayed rounded means. Time/token statistics are computed only over the 7 primary runs with non-null measurements (evals 1, 3, 4, 11, 12, 13, 14); the other 21 evals have null measurements, including the eval 2 re-run and the simulated-transcript/fixture groups (evals 5-10, 15-28). Both eval 26 result entries are nulled and excluded from all aggregates so paired summary calculations stay aligned after the contaminated run.

Evals 12 and 25 updated in v1.6 review pass: added header-model-not-literal-self assertion to both. Both evals remain non-discriminating (1.0/1.0) — general assistants naturally substitute their own model identifier and never print literal 'self' since they have no knowledge of the keyword. Eval 12 now has 5 assertions; eval 25 now has 4 assertions.

Evals 24-28 added in v1.5 review pass. Eval 28 (submodel-splitting) discriminates at +0.33: severity normalization (medium → major) fails without_skill, matching the pattern of evals 5, 7, 8, 9, 10. Evals 24 (rescan-y-response), 25 (pr-url-output), and 27 (branch-not-found-error) are non-discriminating: these behaviors are intuitive enough for a capable baseline to handle correctly. Eval 26 (unsupported-model-error) was contaminated and excluded in this run: the without_skill agent read the SKILL.md from the filesystem and reproduced the skill-defined error message format, so the specific phrasing may still be discriminating but that cannot be determined from this run. Adding evals 24-28 (1 discriminating, 3 non-discriminating, 1 contaminated/excluded) diluted the headline delta from +0.29 to +0.26.

expectations.text: The agent enters consistency mode (not spec mode or diff mode) because the path does not contain plan.md + tasks.md
